response.justification: Wei Chen brings directly relevant experience — current work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq aligns well with the AI-native engineering environment, and the Dunnhumby stint demonstrates genuine high-throughput distributed systems work at scale (millions of HTTP requests per second). Staff-level titling is present in both current and prior roles. However, Python does not appear anywhere in the resume — neither in the skills section nor in any employment narrative — which is a hard gap against a must-have requirement. The distributed systems depth is also somewhat dated (2018–2020), and there is no explicit evidence of on-call ownership, incident response, or cross-functional stakeholder management at the scale this role demands.
strengths: Current hands-on work with agentic workflows, MCP code generators, and AI-agent infrastructure maps directly to the AI-native engineering baseline the role expects
Real high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O) demonstrates the technical depth required
Consistent Staff-level titling and a long track record of breadth across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure show the systems-thinking range the role values
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume — not in skills, not in any role description — which directly conflicts with the 'Strong Python' must-have requirement

High-throughput distributed systems experience is concentrated in a role that ended in 2020; more recent work skews toward product TypeScript and tooling rather than reliability-critical backend systems
No visible evidence of operational ownership (on-call rotations, incident response, monitoring) or explicit cross-functional stakeholder management, both listed as must-have non-technical skills
